mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistical correlation with FEDFUNDS changes over a 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC < 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 5.0%, labor market slack signals deteriorating consumer spending power and recession risk, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >55% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 5.0%, or the signal loses predictive power during expansion phases.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in real personal income and housing starts indicate a synchronized collapse in consumer demand and construction activity, producing negative equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint decline of RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict negative returns, or single-series effects dominate the joint signal in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indexes signal strengthening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which generates positive short-term S&P 500 returns specifically during early-cycle recovery phases.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to predict positive 5-day returns during recovery phases, or the signal reverses direction in late-cycle regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation erodes real corporate margins and forces tighter monetary policy expectations, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL increases show positive or zero correlation with 20-day forward returns across multiple rolling windows, or Rank IC < 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained monthly payroll growth exceeding 150,000 signals robust labor demand and economic expansion, driving positive equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Payroll growth above the threshold fails to generate positive 60-day returns, or the signal becomes statistically insignificant during expansion periods.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding real GDP combined with declining federal funds rates creates a favorable growth-and-easing environment that produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The combination of rising GDPC1 and falling FEDFUNDS does not yield positive forward returns, or the interaction term loses significance in regression tests.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during late-cycle expansions signals capacity constraints and impending economic slowdown, generating negative short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines fail to predict negative 5-day returns during late-cycle phases, or the signal shows no regime dependence in out-of-sample tests.
